notes: Deep read clean: prune/record/deny semantics, per-call window storage, cleanup key-removal (made observable via the wider-window trick, no reach-ins), :infinity path, guards, value-keyed terms all match prompt<->gold<->harness. ONE minor prompt over-promise (T2.6 material, no data change): 'waiting exactly retry_after_ms and calling again must succeed' is false when max_requests VARIES between calls (count can exceed the new max by more than one slot); harness and example only exercise constant-max, gold behavior is reasonable.

Three prompt-precision defects, no behavioral bug (gold<->harness consistent, harness honest about serialization): (1) prompt + state/1 doc promise :half_open as an observable state, but the gold's half-open is transient inside one handle_call — state/1 can NEVER return :half_open, half_open_max_probes is vestigial (its limit branch is dead code under GenServer serialization), and the prompt sentence 'Additional calls beyond the probe limit get {:error, :circuit_open}' is untestable; (2) consecutive-failure semantics (success resets the count) is TESTED (harness line 82) but never stated in the prompt — blind solvability by convention only; (3) gold wraps non-{:ok,_}/{:error,_} returns as {:error, {:unexpected_return, other}} — undocumented AND untested, contradicting 'Return whatever the function returned'. Task A: prompt precision fix, folded into the Kamil sign-off queue. Task B candidate: template rule 'no documented option or state may be unobservable' (inverse of the S9 documented-seams rule).

Two prompt-precision defects, gold<->harness consistent: (1) requests with tokens > capacity can NEVER succeed (refill caps at capacity), yet the gold returns a finite retry_after_ms and the prompt promises unconditionally that waiting retry_after_ms refills enough to satisfy the request — false here; the harness tests the case but wildcards the retry value ({:error, :empty, _}). Contract should either return a distinct error (e.g. :too_large) or document that retry_after is meaningless for unsatisfiable requests. (2) A manual :cleanup message performs the pass AND schedules an extra timer while the periodic one is still pending — sweep cadence doubles permanently; undocumented (001_001 documents the same behavior; here the prompt's additional contract is silent). Both are Kamil sign-off queue material; retry-after over-promise is the same class as 001_001's noted nit but sharper.

REAL gold robustness defect + one minor laxity: (1) an unsatisfiable-but-valid cron (e.g. '0 0 31 4 *' — April has 30 days; also Feb 30/31) passes field validation, then next_run_time's scan walks day/month jumps to the 2.2M iteration cap (~seconds of CPU; the cap comment was sized for minute-stepping) and RAISES inside handle_call — crashing the scheduler and losing all registered jobs. Prompt hole: behavior for never-matching expressions is unspecified. Fix direction (Kamil): reject at registration or document; behavior change => full cascade. Harness has zero impossible-date coverage — invisible to all gates. (2) minor: undocumented 'N/step' syntax (e.g. '5/5') is accepted with non-cron semantics (yields {5} instead of 5,10,...59 or a parse error). Note: AND semantics for day-of-month+day-of-week deviate from real cron but ARE documented in the prompt — no action.

Gold behaviorally sound: per-subscription monitors (forced by the unique-ref contract), sibling demonitor+flush on DOWN handles the multi-monitor reality, wildcard matcher exact per spec, unsubscribe cleanup correct incl. empty-topic removal. MINOR prompt tension (T2.6 note, no action): two sentences are phrased for a one-monitor-per-pid design — 'Demonitor the process when its last subscription is removed' and 'the monitor fires only once' — the latter is false under the ref-per-subscription contract the same prompt mandates (each monitor fires; the gold flushes siblings). Navigable: the unique-ref requirement forces the correct design.

Clean small gold: expiry boundary consistent on both paths (get: clock < expires_at; sweep: now >= expires_at), put resets expiration, lazy delete-on-read present and tested. MINOR uniformity notes (T2.6): (a) the harness sends bare :sweep (3 tests) but the prompt documents :sweep only as the TIMER message — the manual-trigger sentence its siblings got ('a bare :sweep performs one pass — the same work the periodic timer performs') is missing; S9 check-14 passes on the token grep. Blind-solvable regardless (any timer handler accepts the manual send). (b) same latent cadence-doubling as 003_001 if a manual :sweep is sent under an integer interval — unobservable under the harness's :infinity config, undocumented.

Exact match across all three artifacts; the additional-interface contract is precision-register and the gold satisfies every sentence: eager ArgumentError validation in the caller, start_link/0 defaulting, before-count warmup boundary (pushes 1..n warming, n+1 active), slack-skip branch ordering (frozen -> warmup -> stddev<slack -> CUSUM), z against prior mean/stddev with epsilon floor, upward-wins tie rule, alert reset-and-freeze with samples pinned at 0 under frozen pushes, reset keeps the stream known but never creates one, check map has exactly the six promised keys. Welford + CUSUM formulas verbatim. No findings.

Textbook PN-Counter: per-node max merge via Map.merge (absent keys keep the present side's value — correct since G-Counter values are non-negative), value = sum(p)-sum(n), ArgumentError on non-positive amounts raised caller-side, state/1 shape exact. CRDT laws hold by construction. MINOR note: merge/2 raises ArgumentError on malformed remote_state — undocumented extra strictness beyond the prompt, benign.

MINOR severity, robustness class (same family as F22 but for inputs just OUTSIDE the documented contract): the prompt's failure taxonomy covers only raise and {:error, _} returns, and the gold handles exactly those (rescue). A func that THROWS or EXITS kills the unlinked Task.start process before the :task_done send — all waiting callers hang forever (call timeout :infinity) AND the key stays in state, so every FUTURE execute on that key joins the dead wait list: one bad func permanently bricks the key. Fix direction: catch :throw/:exit too, or monitor the task and error-out waiters on DOWN, or document the limitation. Within-contract behavior is exact (dedup, join, normalization table, async execution, clear-after-completion). Harness has no throw/exit coverage (would hang).

Clean: sliding deadline reset on get/update/touch, consistent >= expiry boundary lazy+sweep, lazy delete on access, destroy always :ok, spec-exact session-id formula, :infinity + documented manual :cleanup handled. MINOR recurring family note (T2.6 uniformity): the manual-:cleanup re-schedule side effect is documented in 001_001's prompt but not here (third sibling with the same latent cadence-doubling quirk: 003_001, 006_001, 010_001).

Crash machinery is sound: temporary workers under a linked DynamicSupervisor, manual replacement + monitor rebook on DOWN, busy-crash notifies the awaiting client, queue survives, and OTP signal ordering makes the finish-then-die race safe. Two notes: (a) start_link defaults :name to __MODULE__ — two anonymous pools collide with already_started; every family sibling treats absent :name as unregistered, and the harness engineers around it with unique explicit names (T2.6 uniformity note). (b) a task dispatched to a worker that died right after finishing its previous task is reported {:task_crashed, reason} without ever running (send to dead pid drops; DOWN pops the busy entry) — defensible under the prompt, unspecified; re-queueing would be kinder. FIFO, bounded queue, await selective-receive all exact.

Event-sourcing pipeline exact: validate -> produce -> apply -> append; all validation rules and their orderings match (equal-balance withdrawal allowed; nil-state precedence over invalid_amount). Out-of-contract notes (T2.6 sentences at most): (a) an unknown command tuple crashes the server via FunctionClauseError but ONLY for open accounts — the nil-state catch-all returns {:error, :account_not_open}; asymmetric let-it-crash; (b) non-numeric amounts pass the <= 0 term-order comparison and crash later in apply_event; (c) start_link defaults :name to __MODULE__ (same family deviation as 011_001 — absent :name should mean unregistered).

Flawless against the pinned-behavior contract (July precision register): attempt-0 counting with negative==0 max_retries, no-timer immediate exhaustion, shift saturation at 50, jitter skipped entirely when delay==0, random receives the clamped delay verbatim, clock honestly documented as a no-observable-effect hook, unsupported func shapes DOCUMENTED to crash the server (the exact sentence the April-register roots lack), stateless send_after-carried executions, completion-order replies. Model example for the T2.6 rewrite of siblings.

Intricate but sound: priority+FIFO via per-priority queues, expiry-on-pop with discovery-order recording, processing-flag invariant (false => queues empty) makes drain deadlock-free, task_result/DOWN pair safe under OTP signal ordering, stale results ignored via current_ref match. Out-of-contract notes: (a) a CRASHING processor drops its task silently — recorded neither in processed nor expired (prompt only defines the two lists); (b) expired-but-not-yet-discovered tasks are invisible in status (pending excludes them by clock, expired counts only discovered) — defensible discovery semantics, consistent with expired/1's contract.

Post-repair re-check (prior tranche's 4.1.4 defect fixed): 413 via in-route Parsers.call + RequestTooLargeError rescue with the exact JSON body; validator enforces all three rules with exact error strings (case-insensitive extname, CSV two-lines-or-comma rule incl. trim edge, Jason decode message passthrough); Store stamps proper v4 UUIDs (version/variant bits correct) + ISO8601 UTC; router opts via copy_opts_to_assign; disk name = uuid + original extension; 201 carries all six promised keys. Cosmetic only: naive base_url concatenation would double a trailing slash.

Clean: Bearer parsing exact, 401-halt before :match per prompt, 404->403->action ordering on both endpoints, 409 via add_member_safe (which re-checks not_found — non-atomic with the cond checks but single-process safe), copy_opts_to_assign store plumbing reaches AuthPlug (assign set before the pipeline). Undocumented extras (note only): 400 bad_request for malformed POST bodies (prompt silent), and a conn.private[:team_store] store-resolution channel ahead of the documented :store option.

Faithful: restarting linger window matches the 'full linger_ms with no further message' contract, arrival order preserved via prepend+reverse, selective receive ignores foreign messages, 401 plain-body / 204 empty / batched-JSON shapes exact, Registry duplicate-mode pub/sub per spec, router passes all three opts through copy_opts_to_assign. NOTE (contract-silent): no unsubscribe — a same-process re-poll adds a DUPLICATE registration and doubles deliveries; real per-request processes die so Registry cleans up, but it is a latent test-context hazard the harness must avoid (fresh process or fresh user per poll).

MINOR severity, prompt-gap class: duplicate composite keys WITHIN a list are silently collapsed last-write-wins by Map.new — earlier records vanish from all three result buckets, a lossy semantic for a reconciliation engine whose purpose is complete accounting (reconcile([r1a,r1b], [], key_fields: [:id]) loses r1a without trace). Prompt is silent on duplicates; 26 harness tests have zero coverage. Fix direction (Kamil): specify the semantic (error / last-wins / group) in the prompt, then align gold+harness. Also note: a record MISSING a key field keys as nil and can match another nil-keyed record — the missing-as-nil rule is stated only for compare_fields, unspecified for key fields. Core behavior otherwise exact (tuple composite keys, per-pair field derivation, ==-diff with missing-as-nil, purity).

Clean parser: H2-only heading regex airtight against #/###/indented/no-space variants; item regex requires exactly one leading dash so nested/indented bullets ignore correctly; backtracking keeps mid-text parentheses in the description and takes only the TRAILING parens as tags; trim/reject-empty tag handling; pre-heading items discarded; empty-heading categories kept with items: []; empty input -> []. Edge quirks (regex-consistent, note only): an item whose description is ONLY a parens group parses it as description with tags [] (no whitespace for the tag branch), and any trailing parens are tags by convention even when prose.

Solid: duplicate check first, white/grey/black DFS launched from EVERY id so unrooted cycles are caught, extract_cycle returns top-down ids for self/direct/indirect cycles, cycle error precedes orphan handling regardless of strategy, orphan subtrees drop (or re-root) coherently, root and child order both preserve input order, original fields kept + :children added. The prompt's diamond-shape sentence is vacuous under single-parent_id data (unexpressible) but harmless; children_map append is O(n^2) worst-case — correctness unaffected. (Family history: this root's missing error-variant was the dialyzer gate's planted-regression exemplar — current spec carries both error variants.)
